steps.title: Identify where the independent clauses meet
steps.content: At the blank, the text transitions from one independent clause (ending with “temple walls”) to another (beginning with “he discovered the markings…”).

steps.title: Apply the independent clause separation rule
steps.content:
A period correctly separates two independent clauses, which is what option D provides. Options A, B, and
  • C violate this rule: A uses a comma (comma splice)
  • B uses no punctuation (run-on)
  • C is ungrammatical
. That matches choice D — D is correct.
